tool.name: Beacon2 update record
tool.description: update one or more Beacon records in MongoDB from JSON
tool.command:
@BEACON2_RUNTIME_SETUP@ &&
touch conf/__init__.py &&
cp '$conf_py' 'conf/conf.py' && ln -s '$input_json' 'input.json' &&
update_record.py --file 'input.json' --recordType '${record.record_type}' --collection '${record.collection_name}' > output.log 2>&1

configfiles.configfile:
# Auto-generated by the Galaxy wrapper for Beacon2 CSV to BFF
csv_folder = '.' output_docs_folder = '.' entry_type = '${advanced.entry_type}' only_process_reads_with_allele_frequency = ${advanced.only_process_reads_with_allele_frequency} populations_by_allele_counts = ${advanced.populations_by_allele_counts} reference_genome = '${advanced.reference_genome}' datasetId = '${dataset_id}' case_level_data = ${advanced.case_level_data} exact_heterozygosity = ${advanced.exact_heterozygosity} num_rows = ${advanced.num_rows} verbosity = ${advanced.verbosity}
# Tool-specific record type mapping record_type = '${record.record_type}' collection_name = '${record.collection_name}'
# Database connection database_host = '${advanced.database.database_host}' database_port = ${advanced.database.database_port} database_name = '${advanced.database.database_name}'
# Credentials (exact same logic as your beacon2-import tool) #set $db_auth_source = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_auth_source', "") #set $db_user = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_user', "") #set $db_password = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_password', "") #if $db_user == "" or $db_password == "" or $db_auth_source == "": #set $db_auth_source = "admin" #set $db_user = "root" #set $db_password = "example" #end if
database_user = '$db_user' database_password = '$db_password' database_auth_source = '$db_auth_source'

param.name: input_json
param.label: Input Beacon JSON record or list of records

param.name: record_type
param.label: Record type to validate and update

tool.help:
**Beacon2 update record**
Updates (or inserts) one or more Beacon records directly into MongoDB from a JSON file (The current version doesn't support Runs and Datasets).
The input JSON can contain: - A single Beacon record, or - A list of records (`[ {…}, {…} ]`)
**Features** - Automatically maps the selected **Record type** to the correct MongoDB collection - Validates against the Beacon schema (via the underlying Python tool) - Full support for the new unified configuration system
**New features you can now control (Advanced section):** - Entry type, reference genome, maximum rows - Case-level data, allele frequency & population settings - Exact heterozygosity, verbosity - **Custom `conf.py`** (upload your own file for complete control — highest priority)
MongoDB credentials are read automatically from your Galaxy user preferences.
